known_issues: Code blocks in article content are rendering as a single line, making code hard to read (no proper multi-line display/wrapping).

existing_features.title: Ensure Markdown fenced code blocks (triple backticks) render as true multi-line code blocks (preserve newlines and indentation) in both the Article reader page and the Editor preview, and are not collapsed into a single line.
Improve the article writing experience so users can insert a multi-line fenced code block from the editor toolbar (in addition to inline code), without having to manually type triple backticks.

Harden Markdown code block parsing so fenced code blocks are recognized reliably (including Windows newlines and optional language specifiers like ```js) and still render correctly even if the final closing backticks are missing (fail gracefully).

new_feature_requests.summary: Fix article editor/viewer code blocks so they can display multi-line code properly (not forced into a single line) and improve readability

projectName: Fix multi-line Markdown code block editing and rendering in articles
